title: Intense Sour Brazilian Nectarines Grill
description: A light and sour Brazilian dish featuring grilled nectarines, perfect for a solo dinner, this recipe is jain-friendly and showcases the sweetness of nectarines balanced with a tangy twist.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, ensuring the grates are clean and brushed with oil to prevent sticking.
In a blender or food processor, combine lime juice, cilantro, green chili peppers, ginger, salt, and black pepper, blending until you get a smooth and slightly thick sauce.
Cut the nectarines in half and remove the pits, then brush the cut sides with coconut oil.
Place the nectarines cut side down on the grill and cook for about 5 minutes or until they develop nice grill marks.
Flip the nectarines over and grill for another 5 minutes, until they are tender but still hold their shape.
During the last minute of grilling, brush the nectarines with the prepared sauce, making sure they are evenly coated.
Remove the nectarines from the grill and let them cool slightly before serving.
Serve the grilled nectarines warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired, and enjoy as a light and refreshing solo dinner.
